jokes.text: I'll use that free app that tells what restaurants you're near. You mean the window? Yes.
jokes.setup: Bender mentions using an app to find nearby restaurants
jokes.punchline: Leela points out the obvious solution (looking out the window) which Bender accepts
jokes.explanation: Sharp commentary on over-reliance on technology when simple solutions exist. Perfect economy and great structure.

jokes.text: I am lrrr, ruler of the planet Omicron Persei 8! I demand extra dipping sauce! How many? I don't know. Two?
jokes.setup: Lrrr makes his typical grandiose introduction as ruler of a planet
jokes.punchline: But can only manage to order two dipping sauces uncertainly
jokes.explanation: Classic Lrrr character comedy - mighty alien ruler reduced to mundane fast food ordering decisions.

jokes.text: Welcome to Fishy Joe's. Today is Stroganoff Thursday. We are currently out of the following menu items. Stroganoff.
jokes.setup: Worker announces it's Stroganoff Thursday
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals they're out of stroganoff
jokes.explanation: Classic fast food absurdity - promoting what they don't have. Familiar but well-executed.

jokes.text: I'll have the Stroganoff. Okay.
jokes.setup: Customer orders the stroganoff that was just announced as unavailable
jokes.punchline: Worker accepts the order without comment
jokes.explanation: Simple continuation of the fast food incompetence theme. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: Give me the fried spaghetti-dinner breakfast pocket for lunch, and a big bucket of mixed sodas. One kids' meal. Got it.
jokes.setup: Bender orders an absurdly complex adult meal with specific timing and portions
jokes.punchline: Worker translates it to 'one kids' meal'
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor about fast food translation. The contrast between elaborate order and simple interpretation works well.

jokes.text: Buggalo tots? What exactly are those made out of? You got a warrant?
jokes.setup: Amy asks reasonable question about food ingredients
jokes.punchline: Worker responds as if she's law enforcement investigating a crime
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion suggesting fast food ingredients are so questionable they require legal protection to reveal. Sharp and memorable.

jokes.text: How about the gardener-lover's salad? What kind of cheese filling you want in that? Just give me the fruit cup. What kind of cheese filling you want in that?
jokes.setup: Worker asks about cheese in a salad, which is already questionable
jokes.punchline: Even asks about cheese filling in a fruit cup
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the 'everything has cheese' absurdity. The repetition builds the joke effectively.

jokes.text: Fruit is spelled f-r-o-o-t. And it's got quotation marks around it.
jokes.setup: Leela examines what should be a fruit cup
jokes.punchline: The spelling and quotation marks suggest it's not actually fruit
jokes.explanation: Clever way to suggest fake food ingredients through suspicious spelling and punctuation. Visual comedy that works well.

jokes.text: Heart... at-tack.
jokes.setup: Fry presumably clutches chest dramatically
jokes.punchline: Delivers diagnosis in halting, matter-of-fact way
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry deadpan delivery of medical emergency. Character-appropriate but not particularly original.

jokes.text: I'm fine. Oh! I got a bone in my fruit!
jokes.setup: Fry claims to be fine after apparent heart attack
jokes.punchline: Finds a bone in what was supposed to be fruit
jokes.explanation: Continues the theme of questionable fast food ingredients. The bone in 'froot' caps the food safety concerns nicely.

jokes.text: Aw, mom, do we have to?
jokes.setup: Leela announces they're going to farmers market for healthy food
jokes.punchline: Adult Fry whines like a child, calling Leela 'mom'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's childlike nature. The 'mom' reference fits his relationship with Leela.

jokes.text: You think that's healthy, try this. I found it growing at the bottom of my hamper. Mmm, so fresh and musty. It's what I do.
jokes.setup: Discussion of healthy, fresh food
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg offers something growing in his dirty laundry hamper and is proud of it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment - disgusting but proud of it. The 'It's what I do' is a nice character touch.

jokes.text: Mmm, so fresh and musty. It's what I do.
jokes.setup: Someone apparently tastes Zoidberg's hamper growth
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg takes pride in growing moldy things in his clothes
jokes.explanation: Zoidberg's proud 'It's what I do' makes his gross behavior endearing and funny

jokes.text: That's the kind of sap I like. You the kind of sap I like. I'm scare-roused.
jokes.setup: Amy compliments maple syrup, calling it 'sap she likes'
jokes.punchline: Farmer interprets 'sap' as slang for fool/easy mark, leading to Amy being 'scare-roused'
jokes.explanation: Clever double meaning wordplay with 'scare-roused' being a perfect Amy portmanteau reaction. Well-structured pun.

jokes.text: Listen, pal, I'm looking for a versatile smoke, one that's equally good in a crowded elevator or an audience with the pope.
jokes.setup: Bender wants a versatile tobacco product
jokes.punchline: Specifically wants to be obnoxious in both crowded elevators AND papal audiences
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bender line showing his desire to be maximally obnoxious across all social situations. The specificity makes it brilliant.

jokes.text: Smoke one of these, and God himself will ask to be seated far away from you.
jokes.setup: Farmer needs to top Bender's papal audience reference
jokes.punchline: Claims the tobacco is so offensive even God would avoid the smoker
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that tops Bender's pope reference. Good salesmanship through hyperbole.

jokes.text: I'll bet you run your own hatchery, just you and your wife... Matilda. No, ma'am. Matilda and I gather them eggs in the forest. And Matilda's really more of a mongoose than a wife.
jokes.setup: Amy romantically assumes farmer has wife named Matilda helping with eggs
jokes.punchline: Matilda is actually a mongoose, not his wife
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection subverting Amy's romantic assumptions. The mongoose detail adds nice absurdity.

jokes.text: You're a lucky man. But are they way more expensive than regular eggs? Way more. Ooh! I'll take a dozen.
jokes.setup: Amy asks if eggs are more expensive than regular ones
jokes.punchline: Immediately decides to buy them BECAUSE they're way more expensive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy character moment - the expensive price is a selling point for her wealthy mindset.

jokes.text: Leela blackmailed us all into eating healthy, organic food. Blackmail? What does she have on you? As long as we eat her filthy scrambled eggs, you'll never find out.
jokes.setup: Hermes claims Leela blackmailed them into healthy eating
jokes.punchline: When pressed for details, he maintains the blackmail by not revealing what she has on them
jokes.explanation: Clever recursive joke - he can't reveal the blackmail because it's still working. Good structure.

jokes.text: It means it will hatch into a tiny, little, infant creature unless we cook it first. Hey, Leela, hurry up with those scrambled infants.
jokes.setup: Leela explains that fertile means the eggs will hatch into creatures
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg casually calls them 'scrambled infants'
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark wordplay that reframes breakfast in disturbing terms. Perfect Zoidberg callousness.

jokes.text: What are you, my driver's license?
jokes.setup: Zoidberg is called an inhuman monster
jokes.punchline: He responds as if they're stating obvious information like on an ID
jokes.explanation: Excellent Zoidberg comeback that treats 'inhuman monster' as mundane personal information. Great economy.

jokes.text: I'm going to keep this egg safe and warm and secure until it hatches out into the world. And then I'm going to eat it.
jokes.setup: Fry makes touching speech about protecting the unborn creature
jokes.punchline: Reveals he still plans to eat it after it hatches
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with excellent timing. The moral stance completely undermined by the final reveal.

jokes.text: I'm considering buying a yacht to house my thoroughbreds. How do you expect me to do that without smoking a cigar? That's what I'm gonna call the yacht.
jokes.setup: Bender talks about buying yacht to house thoroughbreds
jokes.punchline: Reveals 'The Unborn Embryo' is just the yacht's name
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that recontextualizes his earlier yacht reference. Typical Bender materialism.

jokes.text: Leggo my eggo, crabbo!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg tries to grab Fry's egg
jokes.punchline: Parodies Eggo waffle slogan while calling Zoidberg a crab
jokes.explanation: Simple commercial parody with species reference. Functional but relies on existing catchphrase.

jokes.text: I was, but you can't sit on something for a week without falling in love with it.
jokes.setup: Fry explains why he changed his mind about eating the creature
jokes.punchline: Universal truth about developing attachment through physical proximity
jokes.explanation: Nice observational humor about bonding and attachment. Relatable truth delivered through absurd situation.

jokes.text: A chance to blossom into a beautiful young man, like I did as a baby.
jokes.setup: Fry makes speech about creature's potential
jokes.punchline: Claims he was a beautiful young man 'as a baby' (temporal confusion)
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry temporal/logical confusion. Good character consistency showing his scrambled thinking.

jokes.text: I was gonna go yachting in those feet!
jokes.setup: Bender sees the creature has dissolved his feet
jokes.punchline: Complains about lost yachting opportunities rather than injury/pain
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender priority system - lifestyle concerns over physical safety. The absurd specificity makes it brilliant.

jokes.text: Relax, you babies. It's no different from stomping a puppy.
jokes.setup: Everyone panics about the dangerous creature
jokes.punchline: Bender offers puppy-stomping as reassuring comparison
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender casual cruelty presented as comfort. Dark but perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I'm standing in a pool of my own feet.
jokes.setup: Bender's feet have been dissolved by the bone vampire
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact description of bizarre body horror situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery of surreal body horror. The calm reporting makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Kill it before someone names it! No! Mr. Peppy just wants to be our friend.
jokes.setup: Amy warns about naming the creature
jokes.punchline: Fry has already named it 'Mr. Peppy' in his very response
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing joke - Amy's warning comes exactly too late. Good structure and execution.

jokes.text: That's what we said about Zoidberg, and look where that got us.
jokes.setup: Discussion about not killing ugly, annoying creatures
jokes.punchline: References keeping Zoidberg as cautionary example
jokes.explanation: Good callback that uses established character dynamics. Treats Zoidberg as ongoing mistake.

jokes.text: Where to, adulterers?
jokes.setup: Taxi driver greeting passengers
jokes.punchline: Assumes they're adulterers based on nothing
jokes.explanation: Random accusatory greeting is absurd and unexpected, fits futuristic taxi driver character

jokes.text: It takes more than that to surprise me... Bender.
jokes.setup: Calculon delivers dramatic line about not being easily surprised
jokes.punchline: The pause before saying 'Bender' suggests he's actually quite surprised
jokes.explanation: Classic Calculon dramatic irony - his acting undermines his claim about not being surprised.

jokes.text: What you doing, little buddy? Uh, yeah, Uncle Bender-- he's your friend.
jokes.setup: Fry treats Mr. Peppy like a pet while Bender watches
jokes.punchline: Bender awkwardly accepts 'Uncle Bender' title despite not wanting to
jokes.explanation: Mild character comedy showing Bender's discomfort with familial roles. Not particularly strong.

jokes.text: Dumb jerk. Come on, get the ball.
jokes.setup: Fry playing fetch with Mr. Peppy
jokes.punchline: He insults the creature while still trying to play with it
jokes.explanation: Fry's frustrated pet owner behavior with a monster, treating it like a disappointing dog

jokes.text: Sweet freak of Mozambique, that thing's colossal!
jokes.setup: Everyone sees Mr. Peppy has grown huge
jokes.punchline: Hermes' characteristic Caribbean-influenced exclamation with unusual geography reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes exclamation with unexpected geographical reference. Great character voice.

jokes.text: How big does one of those things get? Well, that depends on what one of those things is. That seems like a fact worth knowing.
jokes.setup: Amy asks about size, Professor gives unhelpful circular logic
jokes.punchline: Amy points out the obvious flaw in his non-answer
jokes.explanation: Good logical humor pointing out circular reasoning. Amy serves as effective straight man.

jokes.text: It kills its prey by sucking out the skeleton, leaving nothing but a puddle of flesh.
jokes.setup: Professor reads description of creature's abilities
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely specific description of bone-removal death
jokes.explanation: Effectively disturbing visual description. The specificity of 'puddle of flesh' is memorably gross.

jokes.text: Hence, the Latin name Bonus Vampirus, or as you non-catholics would say, the Bone Vampire.
jokes.setup: Professor gives Latin name for creature
jokes.punchline: Incorrectly connects Latin to Catholicism rather than science/taxonomy
jokes.explanation: Good Professor moment showing his confused erudition. The religious/scientific confusion is well-executed.

jokes.text: A single bone vampire, left alone with some quiet music and some bone vampire porn, could produce dozens of deadly offspring. That lucky bastard.
jokes.setup: Professor explains asexual reproduction process with specific details
jokes.punchline: Bender envies the creature's self-sufficient reproductive ability
jokes.explanation: Excellent combination of scientific exposition with Bender's characteristic envy of sexual situations.

jokes.text: Poor, ignorant Leela. The bone vampire is now extinct on its home planet.
jokes.setup: Leela suggests releasing it to live with its own kind
jokes.punchline: Professor condescendingly reveals there are no others left
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor condescension. The 'poor, ignorant' address is perfect character voice.

jokes.text: Its brain is the size of a walnut. So are walnuts, and they're delicious.
jokes.setup: Leela argues creature has no emotions due to walnut-sized brain
jokes.punchline: Bender's non sequitur about walnuts being delicious
jokes.explanation: Perfect non sequitur that completely derails the serious conversation. Great Bender-style logic.

jokes.text: Thanks, Leela, he just needed a little extra push.
jokes.setup: Leela presumably kicks or shoves Mr. Peppy away from Fry
jokes.punchline: Fry frames violent action as helpful assistance
jokes.explanation: Good understatement humor reframing violence as assistance. Typical Fry positivity.

jokes.text: My tanqueraydar is going crazy.
jokes.setup: Bender senses alcohol nearby
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of 'Tanqueray gin' and 'radar' for alcohol detection
jokes.explanation: Clever portmanteau perfectly suited to Bender's alcohol obsession. Good brand specificity.

jokes.text: Thought you could get away from me, huh? You lose again, you stupid intoxicant.
jokes.setup: Bender finds hidden alcohol
jokes.punchline: Addresses alcohol as if it's a sentient adversary in ongoing battle
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bender personification of alcohol as worthy opponent. The 'stupid intoxicant' insult is perfect.

jokes.text: Airwhilka maur fleegle bingo drooper snrk!
jokes.setup: Alien speaks in gibberish alien language
jokes.punchline: Mix of nonsense syllables with 'bingo' randomly inserted
jokes.explanation: Generic alien gibberish with random English word thrown in. Not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: Speak English!
jokes.setup: Alien speaks in foreign language on their own planet
jokes.punchline: Leela demands they speak English despite being visitors
jokes.explanation: Good commentary on tourist entitlement. Simple but effective character moment for Leela.

jokes.text: A small aquarium of whiskey for the busty lass.
jokes.setup: Leela orders the smallest whiskey available
jokes.punchline: Bartender serves 'small aquarium' of whiskey while commenting on her bust
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke about alien drinking standards. The aquarium measurement is absurd and memorable.

jokes.text: A small aquarium of whiskey for the busty lass.
jokes.setup: Leela orders smallest whiskey available
jokes.punchline: Small size is still an aquarium, plus inappropriate comment about her body
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag about scale plus inappropriate bartender behavior, good callback setup

jokes.text: Maczongo. Angus Maczongo. Major Angus Maczongo. Handsome Major Angus Maczongo, Esquire.
jokes.setup: Leela asks for his name
jokes.punchline: Each iteration adds more grandiose titles and self-aggrandizing adjectives
jokes.explanation: Good escalating introduction showing character vanity. The progression builds nicely.

jokes.text: Oh, aye, 'tis a Bonnie time to be an ignorant villager.
jokes.setup: Discussion of prosperity after eliminating bone vampires
jokes.punchline: Character proudly embraces 'ignorant villager' as identity
jokes.explanation: Nice self-aware joke where character embraces typically negative description. Good dialect work.

jokes.text: We're all named Angus.
jokes.setup: Leela confused about which person is named Angus
jokes.punchline: Reveals every villager shares the same name
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist revelation that explains earlier confusion. Great economy and unexpected twist.

jokes.text: The last time I hesitated, a bone vampire snicked out me left shin.
jokes.setup: Angus explains why he doesn't hesitate to kill bone vampires
jokes.punchline: Uses 'snicked' as verb for bone removal, mentions specific body part
jokes.explanation: Good character backstory with creative verb choice. The specificity of 'left shin' adds humor.

jokes.text: My dad always said you could tell a lot about a man by the rigidity of his shins.
jokes.setup: Bender expresses distrust of Maczongo
jokes.punchline: Refers back to shin comment with made-up folk wisdom
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that creates fake folk wisdom about shin rigidity. Great absurdist logic.

jokes.text: Lang a muir a Gryffindor- Slytherin hufflepuff!
jokes.setup: Alien speaks in foreign language during crisis
jokes.punchline: Mixes gibberish with Harry Potter house names
jokes.explanation: Random pop culture reference insertion into alien language. Feels cheap and relies on recognition rather than wit.

jokes.text: He would not, could not to a sheep.
jokes.setup: Fry defends Mr. Peppy against accusations
jokes.punchline: Parodies Green Eggs and Ham rhythm/structure
jokes.explanation: Decent Dr. Seuss parody applied to the situation. Recognizable reference but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Quick, count my bones. There should be 205.
jokes.setup: Leela was attacked by bone vampire and needs assessment
jokes.punchline: Provides exact bone count number for inventory check
jokes.explanation: Good practical response to supernatural attack. The specific number shows Leela's competence/knowledge.

jokes.text: I know, and as much as I love Mr. Peppy, I love Leela more. He has to be... Put down.
jokes.setup: Fry's realization that his pet is dangerous
jokes.punchline: His dramatic pause before euphemistic 'put down'
jokes.explanation: Emotional character moment with pet euthanasia terminology adding gentle humor

jokes.text: I hope you'll consider donating the body to science dinner.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg offers comfort about having to kill Mr. Peppy
jokes.punchline: Combines 'science' and 'dinner' as if they're the same cause
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg line conflating noble cause with his hunger. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Don't worry, I left a trail of bread crumbs. And I left a trail of cigars.
jokes.setup: Leela uses classic fairy tale navigation method
jokes.punchline: Bender's trail is expensive cigars, showing his priorities
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - practical vs wasteful navigation methods

jokes.text: I'll think of you whenever I sit on something round.
jokes.setup: Fry's final goodbye to Mr. Peppy
jokes.punchline: References his egg-sitting period as memorial trigger
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback to the egg incubation period, genuinely touching and funny

jokes.text: The bloody idiot bloody shot me. I'm all bloody bloody!
jokes.setup: Angus is accidentally shot by Fry
jokes.punchline: Uses 'bloody' as both profanity and literal description
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay with British profanity becoming literal. The repetition builds the joke adequately.

jokes.text: Jinkies, he Scooby-Dooed us.
jokes.setup: Revelation that human was disguised as monster
jokes.punchline: References Scooby-Doo formula with appropriate exclamation
jokes.explanation: Appropriate pop culture reference for the situation. Uses 'Scooby-Doo' as a verb effectively.

jokes.text: Mr. Peppy, bullets make you talk? That's not Mr. Peppy.
jokes.setup: Fry thinks the talking is from Mr. Peppy being shot
jokes.punchline: Leela realizes he shot the wrong target
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection reveal, Fry's naive assumption about bullet effects

jokes.text: I work there as a greeter.
jokes.setup: Angus seemed popular at pub during earlier scene
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's just an employee, not actually popular
jokes.explanation: Good callback that recontextualizes earlier scene. Shows his desperation for recognition.

jokes.text: Us greeters don't get a lot of action.
jokes.setup: Angus explains his motivation for elaborate deception
jokes.punchline: Pathetic admission about low-status job and romantic prospects
jokes.explanation: Solidifies his pathetic character motivation. The specificity of 'us greeters' is good detail.

jokes.text: I told you Mr. Peppy's a lover, not a fighter, like Dwayne 'The Rock' 'The Tooth Fairy' Johnson.
jokes.setup: Fry vindicated about Mr. Peppy being gentle
jokes.punchline: References The Rock's tough guy image with his kid-friendly movie role
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference about tough guys doing family films. Not particularly original or clever.

jokes.text: So apparently Major Maczongo and Mr. Peppy were each doing about half the sheep-killing.
jokes.setup: Both the human and creature were killing livestock
jokes.punchline: Leela matter-of-factly divides blame equally
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan summary of the situation. The mathematical approach to blame is funny.

jokes.text: What kind of cheese filling you want in that? All kinds.
jokes.setup: Return to fast food restaurant, worker asks about cheese in everything
jokes.punchline: Bender enthusiastically embraces the absurd cheese obsession
jokes.explanation: Good callback to opening scene running gag. Bender's enthusiastic acceptance works well.
